---
title: "genefiltering"
author: "Kevin Jou"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(Biobase)
library(dplyr)

# --- 1a. Compute row-wise variance on the expression matrix ---
expr_mat <- exprs(eset)  # rows = probes, cols = samples

row_vars <- apply(expr_mat, 1, var)

# Keep top N most variable probes (tune as needed; 500–5000 is typical for PCA)
top_n <- 2000
top_probes <- names(sort(row_vars, decreasing = TRUE))[1:top_n]

# --- 1b. Subset and attach gene symbols ---
expr_var <- expr_mat[top_probes, ]

symbols <- fData(eset)[top_probes, "SYMBOL"]

# Handle probes with missing or duplicated symbols
# Option A: drop probes with no symbol
keep <- !is.na(symbols) & symbols != ""
expr_var <- expr_var[keep, ]
symbols   <- symbols[keep]

# Option B (recommended): collapse duplicate symbols by keeping highest-variance probe
df_var <- as.data.frame(expr_var)
df_var$SYMBOL <- symbols
df_var$var    <- apply(expr_var, 1, var)

df_pca <- df_var %>%
  group_by(SYMBOL) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = var, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-var) %>%
  column_to_rownames("SYMBOL")

# df_pca: rows = unique gene symbols, cols = samples — ready for PCA
```

```{r}
pca_res <- prcomp(t(df_pca), scale. = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(limma)

# --- 2a. Subset eset to labeled samples only ---
labeled_idx <- pData(eset)$group %in% c("good", "bad")
eset_labeled <- eset[, labeled_idx]

group <- factor(pData(eset_labeled)$group, levels = c("good", "bad"))

# --- 2b. Design matrix and contrast ---
design <- model.matrix(~0 + group)
colnames(design) <- levels(group)

cm <- makeContrasts(
  good_vs_bad = good - bad,
  levels = design
)

# --- 2c. Fit model ---
fit  <- lmFit(eset_labeled, design)
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, cm)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2, trend = TRUE)  # trend=TRUE is recommended for log2-RMA data

# --- 2d. Extract all results ---
results <- topTable(
  fit2,
  coef       = "good_vs_bad",
  number     = Inf,
  adjust.method = "BH",
  sort.by    = "P"
)

# results already has fData columns if annotateEset() was run beforehand
# If SYMBOL isn't in results, merge it in:
results$SYMBOL <- fData(eset)[rownames(results), "SYMBOL"]

# --- 2e. Filter for significance ---
sig_results <- results %>%
  filter(
    adj.P.Val < 0.05,
    abs(logFC) >= log2(1.5)   # ~1.5 -fold change in linear scale; adjust to taste
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(SYMBOL) & SYMBOL != "")

# --- 2f. Collapse to gene symbols (keep probe with lowest adj p-val per symbol) ---
sig_results_dedup <- sig_results %>%
  group_by(SYMBOL) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = adj.P.Val, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

# --- 2g. Build the final expression data frame for significant genes ---
sig_probes <- rownames(sig_results_dedup)  # these are still probe IDs at this point

df_sig <- as.data.frame(exprs(eset)[sig_probes, ]) %>%
  mutate(SYMBOL = sig_results_dedup$SYMBOL[match(sig_probes, rownames(sig_results_dedup))]) %>%
  column_to_rownames("SYMBOL")

# df_sig: rows = significant gene symbols, cols = ALL samples (including unlabeled)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)  # for non-overlapping gene labels

# --- Prepare annotation column ---
results <- results %>%
  mutate(
    significance = case_when(
      adj.P.Val < 0.05 & logFC >  log2(1.5) ~ "Up in good",
      adj.P.Val < 0.05 & logFC < -log2(1.5) ~ "Up in bad",
      TRUE                                   ~ "NS"
    ),
    neg_log10_p = -log10(adj.P.Val),
    # Cap extreme p-values for visual clarity
    neg_log10_p_capped = pmin(neg_log10_p, 10)
  )
#check max(results$neg_log10_p) to adjust p-value caps
# --- Label top genes (lowest adj p-val among significant) ---
top_labels <- results %>%
  filter(significance != "NS") %>%
  slice_min(order_by = adj.P.Val, n = 20, with_ties = FALSE)

# --- Color palette ---
sig_colors <- c(
  "Up in good" = "#E63946",
  "Up in bad"  = "#457B9D",
  "NS"         = "grey70"
)

# --- Plot ---
volcano <- ggplot(results, aes(x = logFC, y = neg_log10_p_capped, color = significance)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(
    data = filter(results, significance != "NS"),
    alpha = 0.8, size = 1.6
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-log2(1.5), log2(1.5)), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_label_repel(
    data        = top_labels,
    aes(label   = SYMBOL),
    size        = 2.8,
    max.overlaps = 20,
    box.padding  = 0.3,
    label.padding = 0.15,
    segment.color = "grey50",
    segment.size  = 0.3,
    show.legend  = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = sig_colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(results$logFC)), ceiling(max(results$logFC)), by = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Differential Expression: Good vs. Bad",
    subtitle = paste0(
      sum(results$significance == "Up in good"), " up in good  |  ",
      sum(results$significance == "Up in bad"),  " up in bad"
    ),
    x     = expression(log[2]~"Fold Change (good / bad)"),
    y     = expression(-log[10]~"Adjusted P-Value"),
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "grey40", size = 10),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    axis.line       = element_line(color = "grey30")
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = max(results$logFC) * 0.85, y = -log10(0.05) + 0.15,
           label = "FDR = 0.05", size = 3, color = "grey40")

print(volcano)

```

```{r}
genes_of_interest <- c("INS", "GCG", "PDX1", "PTF1A", "NKX6-1")  # replace with yours

custom_labels <- results %>%
  filter(SYMBOL %in% genes_of_interest)

volcano +
  geom_point(
    data  = custom_labels,
    shape = 21,
    size  = 3,
    color = "black",
    fill  = "gold"
  ) +
  geom_label_repel(
    data         = custom_labels,
    aes(label    = SYMBOL),
    size         = 3,
    color        = "black",
    fill         = "white",
    box.padding  = 0.4,
    fontface     = "bold",
    show.legend  = FALSE
  )
```
